mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 250 in the CPIAUCSL series signals central bank inaction, leading to real rate compression and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflation above 250, or CPIAUCSL fails to predict returns in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust demand conditions, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: 60-day returns in co-expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction term between GDPC1 and HOUST shows negative coefficient in regression models.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market (UNRATE < 3.5%) combined with strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150k) signals inflationary pressure, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days via rate hike expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite labor market tightness, or the interaction term between UNRATE and PAYEMS shows positive coefficient in regression models.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO < 108) indicates weakening supply chain capacity, creating deflationary pressure that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through reduced cost pressures.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite declining INDPRO, or INDPRO fails to predict returns in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS > 510k) during low unemployment (UNRATE < 4%) signals strong demand dynamics, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through corporate revenue growth.
failure_condition: 60-day returns in synchronized demand-supply periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction term between RSAFS and UNRATE shows negative coefficient in regression models.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in unemployment (UNRATE < 0.2pp/mo) signals labor market overheating, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days via inflationary expectations and rate hike fears.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances with declining UNRATE, or UNRATE fails to predict returns in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO > 110) and declining housing starts (HOUST < 1200) signals supply-side strength amid weak demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through sector rotation effects.
failure_condition: 20-day returns in asymmetric supply-demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction term between INDPRO and HOUST shows negative coefficient in regression models.
